EVANS v. USF [SFSC CGC 08-473864]— [PROPOSED] ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF’S MOTION FOR
LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 Plaintiff Jessie Evans’ (“Plaintiff”) motion for leave to file second amended complaint
2 |lcame on for hearing on May 21, 2010, in Courtroom 505 of the above-captioned court, the
3 ||Honorable John K. Stewart presiding. Plaintiff was represented by Dan Siegel and Michael
4 || Siegel. Defendant University of San Francisco (the “University”) was represented by Michael J.

5 || Vartain and Linda K. Adler.

6 Having reviewed all of the papers and considered oral arguments of the parties, and for

7 || good cause showing,

8 IT IS HEREBY ORDERED:

9 Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is denied. Simmons v.
10 || Allstate Insurance Company (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 1068; Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo
11 || Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049, 1054-1055; and Schaffer v. City and
12 || County of San Francisco (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 992, 1005.

13 Plaintiff seeks to amend the pleadings to allege, as the gravamen of two new causes of
14 || action, statements that the Court found to be protected speech in its Order of July 30, 2008
15 || granting the anti-SLAPP motion.

16 “It is the public policy of the State that complaint arising from the exercise of free

speech rights be evaluated at an early stage, this cannot be defeated by filing an

17 amendment, even as a matter of right, pursuant to section 472.” Sylmar Air
18 Conditioning, supra, 122 Cal.App.4th at 1052.

19 If Plaintiff's amendments were permitted at this time, the University would be entitled to
20 || file another anti-SLAPP motion. The case is now not an early stage. It is one month from trial. It
21 || would be prejudicial to permit amendments that would require the University to go through a
22 ||further anti-SLAPP motion. The Court additionally finds that the University would likely
23 || prevail on such a further anti-SLAPP motion.

24 Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is denied.

25 ||SO ORDERED.

26 ||DATED: ___6 | [0 Jul Se

Hon. Judge of the Superior Court
